news.title: Hiranandani forays into senior living, to develop one million sq ft in Chennai
news.para: Hiranandani Communities is venturing into senior living with a new project, 'Elements', in Chennai's Hiranandani Parks, Oragadam. This 4.5-acre development, a ₹300 crore venture with GTB Group, will offer 400 residences starting at ₹60 lakh.  The larger township aims for ₹4,000 crore revenue, building on its existing success.

news.title: Haryana CM emphasises strict action on civic issues in Gurugram
news.para: Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini declared Gurugram the state's fastest-growing city, vowing zero tolerance for laxity in civic amenities. He directed officials to report on water supply, sewerage, and STPs, emphasizing timely delivery of quality basic services to all residents. The CM also ordered stringent action against the tanker mafia and builders failing to provide promised amenities.

news.title: Soon, register sale deeds at any taluka office soon: Goa CM
news.para: Goa citizens will soon be able to register sale deeds at any of the 12 sub-registrar offices across the state, irrespective of the land's location. Chief Minister Pramod Sawant announced that the entire process for marriage and land registrations will be streamlined within 10 days, resolving IT infrastructure issues with NIC and ITG.

news.title: Action taken against illegal commercial constructions in Ludhiana
news.para: Ludhiana Municipal Corporation sealed three illegal shops and a building in Zone B on Monday. Additionally, an under-construction commercial building was demolished near Mohar Singh Nagar for illegal conversion from residential to commercial use. Residents are urged to obtain building plan approvals before commencing construction.

news.title: Total Environment Resorts raises ₹175 crore through NCDs
news.para: Total Environment Resorts has successfully issued ₹175 crore in listed, rated, non-convertible debentures, with Standard Chartered as the sole arranger and green co-ordinator. The proceeds will fund a residential real estate project in Bengaluru, adhering to green bond principles and SEBI regulations.

news.title: Ludhiana civic body plans to take over colonies & sites from Glada
news.para: Ludhiana Municipal Corporation is considering taking over colonies and sites from Glada, a process Glada has attempted for over a decade. This follows a similar ongoing takeover from the Ludhiana Improvement Trust. Officials are holding meetings to finalize the proposal, which was previously rejected by the MC house due to financial concerns.

news.title: Rajasthan HC gives govt final chance to reply on 86 illegal colonies on RHB's land
news.para: The Rajasthan High Court has given the state government a final chance to respond by December 9 in a case involving 86 illegal colonies on Rajasthan Housing Board land. The court expressed strong displeasure over the government's failure to comply with previous orders to reclaim public land, despite the Supreme Court dismissing the state's appeal.

news.title: Maharashtra CM to hand over 423 flats to police families at Naigaum BDD chawls
news.para: Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis will soon hand over keys to 864 flats at Naigaum BDD chawls in Dadar. This marks a significant milestone for police families who have resided there for generations. The completed project includes five wings of 22 storeys each. Beneficiaries will receive ownership of 500-square-foot flats.

news.title: Odisha REAT fines developer for unfair trade practices, denying possession to buyer
news.para: Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal fined a developer Rs 10 lakh, comparing their "dubious" tactics to Shakespeare's Shylock. Despite full payment for a commercial unit, the buyer faced denied possession and demands for extra money. The tribunal ordered possession, interest, and barred further demands, emphasizing the right to shelter.

news.title: Germany’s Bernhard Schulte buys six floors of office space in Mumbai
news.para: Global maritime major Bernhard Schulte Shipmanagement has acquired office space in Mumbai for over Rs 304 crore. This marks a significant acquisition by an international shipping group in India. The new office will serve as the company's India headquarters. It will consolidate ship-management, crewing, technical, and digital operations. The space is scheduled for completion by December 2027.

news.title: Sirca Paints' profit grows 36% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Sirca Paints India has extended its affiliation with Sirca SPA, Italy, ensuring the esteemed brand name will be in use until 2041. The company marked a robust performance in the latest quarter, experiencing a 24% year-on-year increase in revenue to reach Rs 131 crore. Furthermore, net profit surged 36% to Rs 18 crore.

news.title: Developer must fix defects in Andheri building: MahaRERA
news.para: MahaRERA has ordered a developer to fix structural defects in the common areas of the Vasant Oasis Jolan building in Andheri East. This follows a housing society's complaint about poor construction quality and persistent leakage issues. The authority ruled the society is entitled to relief similar to previous cases, directing rectification of all common area defects.

news.title: MahaRERA efficiency up after adopting hybrid mode hearings
news.para: MahaRERA now offers hybrid hearings for housing disputes, allowing complainants and parties to choose between physical or virtual attendance. This move, prompted by a Bombay High Court directive, enhances convenience and transparency, leading to increased participation and more efficient resolution of cases across Maharashtra.

news.title: Saira Banu says row resolved, police close case against Mumbai-based builder
news.para: Actress Saira Banu Khan's cheating and forgery case against builder Sameer Bhojwani has been closed by the Economic Offences Wing. The complainant stated the allegations stemmed from a misunderstanding and that the property dispute has been amicably resolved. A C summary closure report was accepted by the magistrate.

news.title: Noida: FIR against Logix Buildestate & Ace Group after farmers allege assault
news.para: An FIR was filed against two developers for alleged intimidation and assault after a farmer claimed men linked to them forcibly occupied his agricultural land and assaulted family members. Developers Ace and Logix have rejected the allegations, calling the FIR 'false, one-sided' and stating the land was legally acquired.

news.title: Karnataka RERA directs Bengaluru development body to register delayed layout
news.para: Karnataka Rera has ruled the Bangalore Development Authority (BDA) is a "promoter" under the Real Estate Act, ordering it to register the Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Layout within two weeks. This decision offers relief to thousands of homebuyers awaiting possession for nearly a decade, with BDA facing potential penalties for past non-compliance.

news.title: WeWork India's profit plunges to ₹6.4 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: In the latest September quarter, WeWork India reported a notable decline in net profit, landing at Rs 6.4 crore, even as total income surged to Rs 585.54 crore. The CEO emphasized the remarkable revenue growth and improved margins, signaling a robust performance overall.

news.title: Chandigarh to set up 17 more sites for construction waste disposal
news.para: Chandigarh Municipal Corporation is establishing 17 new dedicated sites for construction and demolition waste to address increasing generation. The civic body aims for at least one collection point per ward to facilitate responsible disposal and curb illegal dumping. An agency has been hired to construct these sites, with progress pending site allocation.

news.title: Sharad Malhotra appointed as MD of Nippon Paint India
news.para: In a groundbreaking move, Nippon Paint India has announced the appointment of Sharad Malhotra as the first Indian Managing Director. Starting December 1, 2025, Malhotra is set to steer the company’s vision and expansion strategies in a market that holds great promise for growth and innovation.

news.title: About 11 illegal buildings set to be razed in Bengaluru
news.para: Bengaluru East city corporation has commenced the demolition of 11 illegal structures found with significant deviations from approved building plans. A building in Whitefield with multiple unauthorized floors is currently being dismantled manually. This action follows a survey of over 600 under-construction buildings and the issuance of notices.
